text.title: A/B Testing Ads Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of A/B Testing Ads MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Each test MUST change exactly one variable (offer OR hook OR creative-type OR visual OR copy OR CTA). Multi-variable tests are invalid.

rationale.source: Experimentation literature — Kohavi, Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments

rule.rationale: Multi-variable tests cannot isolate cause; verdicts are uninterpretable.

rule.statement: Required sample size MUST be calculated BEFORE launch using baseline CTR, MDE, alpha=0.05, power=0.8. Post-hoc sizing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Pre-calc prevents peeking + early-stop; post-hoc inflates false-positive rate by 3-5x.

rule.statement: Control and variant MUST receive equal budget and equal audience. Unequal allocation invalidates the verdict.
rule.rationale: Budget skew biases velocity; verdict cannot distinguish budget effect from creative effect.

rule.statement: Verdict MUST require >= 95% confidence (or pre-defined alpha). Sub-95% verdicts are 'inconclusive', not 'winner'.
rule.rationale: Below 95% the false-positive rate makes verdicts effectively noise.

rule.statement: Every test MUST log verdict (winner / inconclusive / null), p-value, sample-size achieved, learnings, where_applicable.
rule.rationale: Unlogged verdicts evaporate; team cannot build cumulative knowledge.

rule.statement: Tests MUST follow priority order offer → hook → creative-type → visual → copy → CTA. Testing CTA before offer is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Higher-leverage variables tested first yield bigger lift; CTA-first wastes budget on noise.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
